what_it_does: Implements a React workspace for browsing and editing studio-wide design decisions. It shows a master list of decisions and a detail panel with live component specimens for each option; selecting an option calls setTweak to apply the choice globally.
observations: Uses window.MS_DECISIONS as the catalog and active-option resolver.
Exports window.DSSpecimen so other surfaces can reuse the same option preview renderer.
Includes dedicated specimen components for radius, density, warmth, accent, motion, voice, and a generic label fallback.
Motion preview renders an SVG cubic-bezier curve and a CSS-transitioned runner.
Tracks how many decisions differ from their recommended setting and offers a reset button.
Mobile layout uses a boolean mobileDetail flag to toggle between list and detail views.
Relies on external globals such as Icon, Eyebrow, Btn, ViMark, and window.MS_TOAST.
standouts: Live component specimens under every option make abstract tokens visually concrete.
Single tweak commit path: each option maps to a tweak value written through props.setTweak.
SVG bezier path derived from a CSS cubic-bezier() string for motion preview.
Shared DSSpecimen exported on window for cross-surface consistency.
conventions: Functional React components with props destructuring.
Global module sharing via window.* assignments.
BEM-ish class naming with dsys and dspec prefixes.
Local state hooks aliased as useStateDS/useEffectDS.
requestAnimationFrame used to restart the motion preview animation.
concerns: Heavy reliance on undefined-in-file globals makes the component's contract implicit.
cubic-bezier parsing regex will fall back to a straight line for any non-matching curve string.
mobileDetail state alone does not enforce actual viewport breakpoints.
useEffectDS is imported but never used.
The X and Y helpers are local closure functions, not components, despite being listed as symbols.
notes: A preview-driven design-system control panel that turns token choices into rendered mini-compositions and commits them through a single tweak setter.
questions: Where are window.MS_DECISIONS, Icon, Eyebrow, Btn, ViMark, and window.MS_TOAST defined?
What is the exact shape of a decision and its options?
How is the mobile breakpoint detected and synchronized with mobileDetail?
When and how does setTweak persist or broadcast the change across the app?
How does window.MS_DECISIONS.radiusVars compute the radius CSS variables?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file exposes open design decisions with live previews and writes the chosen tweak through a single setter, as described in the header comment.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for decisions
Global application through minimal tweak surface
intention_signals: Make design-system configuration tangible by rendering each option as a real mini-composition, guide users toward recommended defaults, and keep every decision committed through one shared tweak mechanism.
novelty: A shared specimen renderer that previews design options with actual component styling, including an SVG cubic-bezier curve derived from CSS timing functions.
contribution: A reusable design-decision workspace with master/detail navigation, per-option live specimens, active/recommended indicators, and a single tweak-commit path for applying choices studio-wide.
summary_for_embedding: React workspace component that renders a master/detail design-decision interface with live component specimens for each option and commits the selected tweak via setTweak.
intention_for_embedding: To let humans see and choose design-system options and apply them globally through a single tweak mechanism.

symbols.does: Renders a mini-composition showing how the current radius option affects cards, chips, buttons, and inputs.
symbols.description: Applies the option's radius multiplier locally through window.MS_DECISIONS.radiusVars so the preview reflects the --r-* ramp without changing global state.

symbols.does: Renders a surface preview using the padding value that corresponds to the chosen density option.
symbols.description: Maps the density token to a pixel padding via DS_DENSITY_PAD and displays a panel with placeholder content plus a caption.

symbols.does: Renders a paper/card preview with a bronze wall wash whose opacity depends on the warmth value.
symbols.description: Computes wash opacity as Math.max(0, (value - 0.7) * 0.24) and overlays it on a paper element to visualize warmth.

symbols.does: Renders a button, chip, and link using the raw accent hex to preview the color.
symbols.description: Uses the option value directly as fill, border, and text color so the user sees the accent before the full palette derivation runs.

symbols.does: Renders an SVG easing curve and a moving runner to preview a motion timing option.
symbols.description: Displays the cubic-bezier curve as an SVG path and animates a runner along a track using the curve as the transition timing function.

symbols.does: Converts a CSS cubic-bezier() string into an SVG path inside a 60x60 box.
symbols.description: Parses the cubic-bezier arguments, maps control points into the preview coordinate space with inverted Y, and falls back to a straight line if parsing fails.

symbols.does: Local helper inside dsBezierPath that maps a normalized parameter t to an X coordinate.
symbols.description: Computes 2 + t * 56 to place bezier points within the 60x60 preview box.

symbols.does: Renders a chat-bubble specimen containing a sample phrase for the selected voice option.
symbols.description: Shows a bubble with the Vi mark and the option's sample text so the tone of voice can be judged visually.

symbols.does: Fallback specimen that displays the option label as a pill.
symbols.description: Used for decisions without a bespoke preview renderer, such as navigator join, wall texture, or generation breadth.

symbols.does: Dispatches to the correct specimen component based on decision.preview.
symbols.description: Acts as a single preview router for radius, density, warmth, accent, motion, voice, and label specimens, and is exposed on window for reuse.

symbols.does: Renders the detail view for a single design decision with its options and specimens.
symbols.description: Shows the decision question, rationale, affected tokens, option grid with live previews, active/recommended flags, and a reset-to-recommended control.

symbols.does: Commits the selected option by calling setTweak and shows a confirmation toast.
symbols.description: Skips if the option is already active, otherwise writes the option value to the decision's tweak key and notifies via window.MS_TOAST.

symbols.does: Triggers the motion preview animation for the current detail panel.
symbols.description: Resets the racing state, forces a layout frame, then enables the runner animation and disables it after 900ms.

symbols.does: Renders the navigable master list of design decisions grouped by category.
symbols.description: Lists decisions with their active option label, highlights the selected decision, and shows a dot when a decision differs from its recommended setting.

symbols.does: Selects a decision and switches to the detail view on mobile.
symbols.description: Updates selId and sets mobileDetail to true so the master list is hidden and the detail panel is shown on narrow layouts.
